etymology.description: semantic 金(金) ('metal; money') + phonetic 旁(旁)
etymology.interpretation: 鎊 (bàng, 'pound') is a phono-semantic compound where 金(金) (jīn, 'metal; money') indicates its relation to currency or metal (as coins are made from metals), and 旁(旁) (páng) provides the sound. It reflects a currency unit 'pound' through its phonetic element combined with the metal representation.
etymology.simplification: 鎊(鎊) (bàng, 'pound') simplifies to 镑(鎊) by replacing 金(金) (jīn, 'metal; money') with the simplified radical 钅(鈅, 'metal'). This reduces the number of strokes while maintaining the phonetic component.

examples.chinese: 他存了许多英镑(他存了許多英鎊)。
examples.english: He saved a lot of pounds.

examples.chinese: 她用五十镑买了一件新衣服(她用五十鎊買了一件新衣服)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng wǔshí bàng mǎile yī jiàn xīn yīfú.
examples.english: She bought a new piece of clothing for fifty pounds.

examples.chinese: 在市场上(在市場上)，英镑的价值有所波动(英鎊的價值有所波動)。
examples.pinyin: Zài shìchǎng shàng, yīngbàng de jiàzhí yǒu suǒ bōdòng.
examples.english: In the market, the value of the pound fluctuates.
